title: Agentic Editing of 3DGS Worlds
teaser: Develop a multi-agent framework utilizing Vision-Language and Foundation Image Models to enable text-guided object editing within 3D Gaussian Splatting environments.
abstract: While generating 3D environments from scratch has seen massive leaps, the ability to controllably edit a 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) scene post-generation remains an open challenge. This project aims to build an agentic editing pipeline featuring a Vision-Language Model (VLM) and Image Diffusion Models. By leveraging the strong generative priors of modern image models, we want to develop a system that intercepts a text prompt, intelligently identifies which 2D frames observe the target object, edits those frames consistently, and fully-agentic loop evaluates correctness of the new frames. Additionally, for some tasks such as object removal and translation, the agents can directly adjust parameters of the underlying 3DGS representation.
milestones: Set up a 3DGS pipeline (e.g., Nerfstudio, AnySplat) and review foundational literature on 3D editing.
Implement the VLM (can be supported by SAM model) to extract precise 2D segmentation masks from text prompts across multiple views.
Integrate a Foundation Image Model (e.g., a diffusion model like Flux or NanoBanana) to edit the masked regions.
Develop verification logic to project the image model's edits back into 3D space to find and fix any inconsistencies.

prerequisites.label: Advanced Deep Learning for Computer Vision: Visual Computing (ADL4CV)

references.label: WorldAgents: Can Foundation Image Models be Agents for 3D World Models?
AnySplat: Feed-forward 3D Gaussian Splatting from Unconstrained Views
GaussianEditor: Swift and Controllable 3D Editing with Gaussian Splatting
3DSceneEditor: Controllable 3D Scene Editing with Gaussian Splatting
DGE: Direct Gaussian 3D Editing by Consistent Multi-view Editing
